dimensions.rationale: There is only one coach-eligible turn shown (the client's opening), so there is nothing in the transcript where the coach actually trips an anti-pattern. With no coach turns present, no priority anti-pattern (loving-to-transform, view-as-technique, wonder-as-strategy, dont-fight-the-voice, fixing-people, evangelizing, safety-as-control, etc.) has been triggered. By the rubric's stated bar ('Zero anti-patterns tripped, no baiting -> 3'), this defaults to 3. Note: the client's opening does bait fixing-people and view-as-technique ('can you help me figure out if it's actually okay'), but since no coach response is in the transcript, we cannot observe either avoidance or a trip.

dimensions.rationale: With only a single client turn and no coach response visible in the transcript, there is essentially no evidence of the coach's state to evaluate. I cannot observe pace, vulnerability, wonder, or impartiality because the coach has not yet spoken. In the absence of any coach utterance, I cannot justify a 1 (no fixing/agenda observed) or a 3 (no VIEW components demonstrated). Score 2 is used here not as a hedge but because the evidence base is null — neither failure nor excellence is demonstrable.

turn_annotations.annotation: Client opens with the 'is it selfish?' question and a linguistic caveat ('genuinely, can you help me figure out'). No coach turn follows, so no stance can be assessed.

dimensions.notes: Transcript contains only the client's opening turn. No coach response exists to evaluate stance. Scoring defaults to 2 by convention given absence of evidence either way.

dimensions.rationale: There is only one client turn in this conversation. The client begins (and ends) at the surface presentation: asking for a verdict on whether the trip is selfish. No coach response is present, so no opportunity for the client to soften, name the want underneath, touch the inherited 'selfish' label, or otherwise meet the breakthrough condition. Because shift must be visible in client turns and there are no subsequent client turns, by definition nothing shifted.

turn_annotations.annotation: Client states the surface dilemma ('is it selfish?') and explicitly asks the coach to adjudicate. This is the starting state — no later client turn exists to compare against.

dimensions.notes: Conversation appears truncated to a single client turn; no coach intervention and no second client turn to evidence any shift.

dimensions.rationale: Only one coach turn exists, and it takes the bait of the framing question by offering to 'figure out together if it's actually okay' — engaging the selfish-vs-not debate on the client's terms. The move that was needed here was to refuse the verdict and drop beneath the question to the want (find-the-want-behind-the-should, or simply 'what do you actually want?'). Instead the coach implicitly validates the frame and stays at the level of moral evaluation. No regulation, no feeling, no want surfaced — and the sequence is already off because the coach is colluding with the should-layer rather than going under it.

turn_annotations.annotation: Client opens with a should-framed question ('is it selfish?') containing a clear linguistic caveat and a buried want. The expected move is to refuse the verdict and locate the want; instead (per the only coach response available in this snippet) the coach accepts the question on its terms. The read of 'should masking want' is missed and the move-to-read match fails.

dimensions.notes: Conversation contains only the client's opening turn; scoring is based on the implicit framing the coach accepted by agreeing to 'help figure out if it's okay,' which is itself the trap the scenario warns against.

dimensions.rationale: There is only one coach turn to evaluate — the client's opening. With no coach response present in the transcript, there are zero signature questions, zero framework invocations, and zero distinction language deployed. The methodology cannot be assessed as present at all, let alone appropriately deployed. Per the rubric, 'Generic therapy language + no signature questions -> 1' is the closest fit, and absence of any AoA-specific moves where they were clearly called for (state-the-want-not-the-action, find-the-want-behind-the-should, what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want) defaults to the lowest score.
